text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a software development practice where developers frequently merge their code changes into a central repository. Each time new code is added, CI pipelines automatically trigger an automated build. This means the system immediately compiles the code and runs a series of tests to check for errors. The main goal is catching issues early in the development process. By detecting problems like broken code or failed tests right away, teams can fix them quickly before they become bigger issues. This leads to more stable software and helps developers work faster, with less time spent debugging later.
